title: Backing Up Your Data
drDigitalIntro: A backup is a copy of your important files stored somewhere safe. If your laptop breaks, gets stolen, or is hit by ransomware, a backup lets you recover everything. Time Machine backs up your Mac automatically to an external hard drive. iCloud backs up photos and documents online. The rule is 3-2-1: 3 copies of your data, on 2 different types of storage, with 1 offsite. Even one backup is much better than none.

playgroundTask.question: Your Mac is stolen. You have no backup. What happens to all your photos and documents?

options.text: Apple can recover them for you from their servers
They are gone permanently — there's no way to get them back
They are automatically saved in your email
The police can extract them from the stolen laptop

drDigitalSuccess: Without a backup, lost data is usually gone forever. Time Machine or iCloud can save you from this — back up regularly!
drDigitalHint: If the only copy of your data was on the stolen device, and no one else has a copy...
